goal: Certification: server-side gate evaluation, hybrid checklist, phase transition trigger

acceptance_criteria.description: Refactor POST /api/v1/migration/engagements/{id}/certify to auto-evaluate computable checklist items from DB state. The handler calls db.GetGateMetrics(db, engagementID) to retrieve live reconciliation gate_score, p1_unresolved_count, quality_min_score, and mapping_agreed_pct. Three checklist items are auto-evaluated: (1) recon_score — gate_score >= 0.95, (2) p1_resolved — p1_unresolved_count == 0, (3) parallel_duration — at least one parallel_run with status COMPLETED exists for this engagement (query migration.parallel_run). Two items remain human-attested via request body: (4) stakeholder_signoff (boolean), (5) rollback_plan (boolean). If any auto-evaluated item fails, return 422 with a structured error listing which items failed and their actual values. If human-attested items are missing or false, return 422 listing which attestations are missing.

CertificationRecord model updated: add auto_evaluated JSONB field (stores the server-computed values at certification time — gate_score, p1_count, quality_min_score, mapping_agreed_pct, parallel_run_count — as an immutable snapshot so auditors can see what the system evaluated). The existing gate_score and p1_count fields remain for backward compat but are now populated from auto-evaluation, not from the request body. CreateCertification updated to accept and store the auto_evaluated snapshot.

Successful certification triggers engagement phase transition to COMPLETE via db.UpdateEngagementStatus(db, engagementID, 'COMPLETE'). The certification handler verifies the engagement is currently in PARALLEL_RUN or RECONCILING status before proceeding — returns 409 Conflict if engagement is in any other phase. A gate_transition record is also inserted (from_phase=current, to_phase=COMPLETE, direction=ADVANCE, gate_metrics=auto_evaluated snapshot, authorized_by=actor from JWT).

GET /api/v1/migration/engagements/{id}/certifications (plural) returns paginated certification history — all certification records for the engagement, ordered by created_at DESC. Supports ?page= and ?per_page= (default 20, max 100). Returns {records: [], total: N, page: N, per_page: N}. The existing GET /certification (singular) endpoint remains for backward compat, returning only the latest.

GET /api/v1/migration/engagements/{id}/certification/checklist returns the current checklist evaluation state WITHOUT creating a certification. This is a read-only preview endpoint for the UI to show which items pass/fail before the user submits. Returns {items: [{key, label, auto_evaluated, value, passes, threshold}], all_pass: bool}. Auto-evaluated items show their live computed values. Human-attested items show {auto_evaluated: false, value: null, passes: false}.

Audit integration: successful certification logs to audit_log (if h.Audit is non-nil) with entity_type='certification', action='certify', after_state containing the full certification record including auto_evaluated snapshot. Failed certification attempts (422 responses) also log with action='certify_rejected' and after_state containing the failure reasons. Actor from JWT context.

critical: Auto-evaluated items computed from DB state via GetGateMetrics — NEVER from request body
gate_score and p1_count on CertificationRecord populated server-side, not client-provided
Phase transition only from PARALLEL_RUN or RECONCILING → COMPLETE — 409 on any other status
gate_transition record inserted alongside certification — provides audit trail of the phase change
Human-attested items (stakeholder_signoff, rollback_plan) still required from request body — system cannot auto-evaluate governance decisions
Auto-evaluated snapshot stored as immutable JSONB — auditors see exactly what the system computed at certification time
high: Checklist preview endpoint is read-only — no side effects, no certification record created
Failed certification attempts logged to audit_log with rejection reasons
Parallel run completion check queries migration.parallel_run WHERE engagement_id AND status = 'COMPLETED'
Backward compat: GET /certification (singular) still works, returns latest
422 error response includes structured details of which items failed and their actual values
medium: Threshold constants (gate_score >= 0.95, quality >= 0.70, mapping >= 80%) defined as named constants, not magic numbers
Certification record count per engagement is typically < 10 — no index needed beyond engagement_id FK
